name: Nevada (United States)

colors.symbolism: the silver of Nevada, the Silver State

description: A solid cobalt-blue field. In the upper hoist quadrant a silver five-pointed star sits between two crossed sprays of sagebrush, with a gold scroll bearing the words 'Battle Born' above and the word 'Nevada' in silver below.
history: Nevada's first state flag was adopted in 1905. The current design, by Louis Schellbach III, was the winning entry in a 1926 statewide contest and was adopted in 1929. A 1991 amendment moved the word 'Nevada' below the star to correct a longstanding discrepancy with the legal description.

features.role: the words 'Battle Born' on a gold scroll above the star
features.symbolism: Nevada was admitted to the Union on 31 October 1864 during the American Civil War
